cards.hook: Big Tech didn't grow. It bought its way there.
cards.caption: Big Tech didn't grow big. It bought its way there — hundreds of acquisitions, quietly. You use half of them and never knew. The buried context my feed keeps for you.
cards.stance: Most 'innovation' at scale is just acquisition with a press release.

cards.hook: 350+ mergers by Google and Facebook. Zero blocked.
cards.caption: Google and Facebook made 350+ mergers — not one raised a serious antitrust flag. That's not a loophole, that's the whole game. The deep context my feed surfaces before the timeline.
cards.stance: Antitrust didn't fail Big Tech — it was never allowed to touch it.

cards.hook: Microsoft is racing ahead while Big Tech pulls back on M&A.
cards.caption: Everyone else is slowing acquisitions this year — Microsoft is racing ahead. When one giant buys while the rest freeze, watch what they're building. Signal my feed caught early.
cards.stance: The M&A slowdown isn't caution — it's everyone waiting to see what Microsoft eats next.

cards.hook: The biggest Big Tech acquisitions, ranked.
cards.caption: The largest deals Big Tech ever made — ranked in one place. Half the apps you open daily started as someone else's startup. The map my feed keeps so you see the whole board.

cards.hook: Why don't Big Tech companies actually compete?
cards.caption: Antitrust, lobbying, dark patterns, selling at a loss to kill rivals — why don't Big Tech giants really compete? A question worth sitting with. The threads my feed keeps for you.
cards.stance: 'Free market' and 'Big Tech' haven't been in the same sentence honestly for a decade.

cards.hook: 350+ Big Tech mergers. Zero raised alarms.
cards.caption: 350+ mergers by Facebook and Google — not one got serious scrutiny. The roots of Big Tech run deeper than you think. The stuff my feed keeps before it trends.
cards.stance: Antitrust didn't fail Big Tech — it never showed up.

cards.hook: Every acquisition GAFAM ever made, mapped.
cards.caption: Someone graphed every known Big Tech acquisition into one giant web. It's bigger than you'd guess. This is the deep-dive stuff my feed surfaces before the timeline.

cards.hook: Big Tech isn't racing AI. It's swallowing it.
cards.caption: Amazon and Microsoft already wrote the playbook for consolidating the AI industry. The startups you cheer for are exit strategies. Found before the timeline caught it.
cards.stance: There is no independent AI industry — just Big Tech's farm team.

cards.hook: The AI boom's new exit strategy: founder drain.
cards.caption: Inflection, Adept, Character — all gone the same way. Big Tech skips the merger and just takes the founders. A quiet new playbook my feed caught early.
cards.stance: Acqui-hires are mergers with the antitrust part deleted.

cards.hook: The FTC is finally reopening old Big Tech deals.
cards.caption: Years of untouched acquisitions — now the FTC wants a second look. If they unwind even one, the map changes. The regulatory stuff my feed keeps.

cards.hook: FTC votes 5-0 to reopen Big Tech's past mergers.
cards.caption: Unanimous. Special orders to review deals by Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Facebook. The mergers nobody stopped are back on the table. Caught before the timeline.

cards.hook: An AI CEO is choosing: sell to Big Tech, or die.
cards.caption: Aurora's cash runway is closing and the only exits are spinout, layoffs, or absorption. This is how the AI shakeout actually ends. Found before the headlines.
cards.stance: Most AI startups won't fail loudly — they'll just get eaten.

cards.hook: Everything you believe about Big Tech antitrust is wrong.
cards.caption: A myth-by-myth breakdown of what antitrust actually does — and doesn't — to Big Tech. Sharpens every hot take you're about to post. The stuff my feed keeps.

cards.hook: Big Tech antitrust aftermath and the SV M&A clampdown
cards.caption: A bare YouTube link with no headline payload and no contrast. Nothing to hook a tech reader on.

cards.hook: Apple aside, Amazon Facebook and Google are (almost) all guilty
cards.caption: Apple aside, the antitrust case says Amazon, Facebook and Google are (almost) all guilty. The platforms you build on are the ones on trial. Found before the timeline caught it.
cards.stance: The companies hosting your app are the same ones under antitrust fire.

cards.hook: Did Big Tech antitrust pick the wrong target?
cards.caption: Near-duplicate of card-21, which names Apple explicitly and hits harder. Drop this weaker version to avoid repeating the same post.

cards.hook: The DOJ's top antitrust cop says Big Tech looks like Standard Oil
cards.caption: The DOJ's top antitrust cop says Big Tech now looks a lot like Standard Oil. When regulators reach for the monopoly playbook, your whole stack is in scope. The stuff my feed keeps.
cards.stance: The Standard Oil comparison means breakups, not fines, are on the table.

cards.hook: Everyone blamed Facebook. Maybe Apple was the problem all along.
cards.caption: Everyone thought Facebook was the 800-pound gorilla. This argues Apple was the real monopoly all along. Contrarian take on who Big Tech antitrust should actually target. Found before the timeline.
cards.stance: Apple's App Store lock-in is a bigger monopoly story than the ad giants.

cards.hook: The FTC's Amazon case is a test of antitrust's reckoning
cards.caption: Dated 2023 news-of-record piece with a soft, hedged hook and no sharp contrast. Too stale and low-energy for a viral repost.

cards.hook: Godwin: lessons for Congress from the Big Tech hearing
cards.caption: Inside-baseball Congressional-process commentary. Off-niche for a dev/researcher audience and no contrarian payload.

cards.hook: Companies are firing their SaaS bills and hiring two SWEs instead
cards.caption: Companies are killing giant SaaS bills by in-housing a couple SWEs to build the 10% they actually use. That's where the new tech jobs are hiding. The signal my feed keeps.
cards.stance: Most SaaS spend buys features you never touch. In-housing is the next wave.

cards.hook: Biden's antitrust team is forming — Big Tech just got warned.
cards.caption: Biden's antitrust team is taking shape and Big Tech got the warning shot. Regulation is coming for the giants you build on. The policy news my feed catches before the timeline.
cards.stance: The next platform shift won't be a product — it'll be a court ruling.

cards.hook: Congress promised to regulate Big Tech. It's quietly dying.
cards.caption: 218 upvotes, 464 comments: Congress' push to regulate Big Tech is fizzling out. The giants outlasted the outrage again. The story my feed surfaces while the timeline moves on.
cards.stance: Every Big Tech reckoning ends the same way: a hearing, a headline, then nothing.

cards.hook: Big Tech is quietly walking back the AI jobs apocalypse.
cards.caption: A year ago every exec swore AI would wipe out your job. Now Big Tech is backpedaling hard. The narrative flip my feed caught before the timeline noticed.
cards.stance: The AI jobs apocalypse was a fundraising pitch, not a forecast.

cards.hook: The algorithms rewarding lies also reward the wildfires.
cards.caption: Big Tech's attention economy pays to broadcast lies no matter the cost — and the same engines quietly reward climate disinfo. The uncomfortable link my feed keeps.
cards.stance: The recommendation algorithm isn't neutral — it's optimized for whatever burns hottest.

cards.hook: Big Tech and authoritarians run the same playbook: control what you see.
cards.caption: Both authoritarians and Big Tech want to control the narratives you see — so you never notice them amassing power. The take my feed keeps over the noise.
cards.stance: Feed control is power control. Whoever owns your timeline owns your attention.

cards.hook: Big Tech is hiding the true cost of AI in its balance sheets.
cards.caption: Hidden data center costs are quietly inflating Big Tech's operating income — legal, but it warps how you value AI. The accounting story my feed surfaced early.
cards.stance: AI's real cost isn't on the earnings call — it's buried in the footnotes.

cards.hook: The EU isn't waiting — Big Tech antitrust enforcement is live.
cards.caption: 92 likes, 107 replies: while the US stalls, the EU pushes ahead on Big Tech antitrust. The giants you build on are getting reined in overseas first. Caught before the timeline.
cards.stance: The EU regulates Big Tech while the US holds hearings. Guess who wins.
